question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 46.00% on 2026-02-12 for the Metaculus question "Will an AI-created song chart in the top 20 of the Billboard Hot 100 before 2027?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP for the AI Billboard Hot 100 question will be strictly above 46% on Feb 12. The current CP is 0.48 (just 2pp above threshold), but recent readings cluster heavily at exactly 0.46. Monte Carlo simulations give 0.45-0.58 depending on drift assumptions. The CP sits right at the decision boundary, making this genuinely uncertain. I give a slight edge to YES (55%) because the current position is above the threshold (status quo), there's been an upward trend from mid-January lows, and AI music on Billboard is an accelerating trend that could push forecasters upward. However, the very thin buffer and tendency to cluster at 0.46 keeps this close to a coin flip.

factors.description: Current CP (0.48) is above the 0.46 threshold - status quo favors YES
CP has trended upward from 0.35 in mid-Jan to 0.48 now, suggesting medium-term upward pressure
Last 10 readings: 7/10 at exactly 0.46 (would NOT resolve YES), only 3/10 strictly above. CP is clustering right at the boundary
Monte Carlo with empirical negative drift gives P=0.45, but zero-drift model gives P=0.58. True drift uncertain.
878 forecasters on underlying question - large base provides stability but CP still volatile (ranged 0.35-0.55 in last month)
AI songs on Billboard are an accelerating trend (Billboard coverage), which could push new forecasters to update upward

**Key reasoning:**

This is a meta-prediction about whether the CP will be strictly above 46% on Feb 12, 2026. The critical data:

- **Current CP: 0.48** — just 2pp above the 0.46 threshold
- **Last 10 readings**: 7/10 at exactly 0.46 (which would NOT satisfy "higher than 46%"), only 3/10 strictly above
- **Last 30 readings**: 23/30 strictly above 0.46 — but the most recent readings cluster at the boundary
- **Trend**: Upward from ~0.35 in mid-January to ~0.48 now, but the CP peaked at 0.55 in late January and has been declining
- **Monte Carlo**: Gives 45-58% depending on drift assumptions

The CP is sitting right on the knife's edge. A slight edge to YES because:
1. Status quo (0.48) is above threshold
2. Medium-term upward trend from mid-Jan lows
3. AI music on Billboard is an accelerating phenomenon that could draw upward forecaster updates

But the thin 2pp buffer and heavy clustering at exactly 0.46 keep this near a coin flip.
